1.text: I, Nephi, was born to good parents, so I was taught in the knowledge of my father. I have experienced many hardships in my life, but I have also been greatly blessed by the Lord throughout my days. I have gained a deep understanding of the goodness and mysteries of God, and so I am writing this record of my life.
I am writing this record in the language of my father, which combines the learning of the Jews and the language of the Egyptians.
And I know that the record I am making is true. I am writing it with my own hand, and I am writing it according to my knowledge.

In the first year of the reign of Zedekiah, king of Judah (my father, Lehi, had lived in Jerusalem his whole life), many prophets came that same year, telling the people they must repent or the great city of Jerusalem would be destroyed.
Because of this, my father, Lehi, went out and prayed to the Lord with all his heart on behalf of his people.
And as he prayed to the Lord, a pillar of fire appeared and rested on a rock in front of him. He saw and heard many things, and because of what he saw and heard, he shook and trembled greatly.
After this, he returned to his home in Jerusalem and threw himself on his bed, overcome by the Spirit and the things he had seen.
Being overcome by the Spirit, he was carried away in a vision. He saw the heavens open, and he saw God sitting on his throne, surrounded by countless hosts of angels singing and praising their God.
And he saw One descending from the middle of heaven, whose brightness was greater than the sun at midday.
He also saw twelve others following him, and their brightness surpassed the stars in the sky.
They came down and went across the earth. The first one came and stood before my father, gave him a book, and told him to read it.
And as he read, he was filled with the Spirit of the Lord.

And he read, saying: Woe, woe to Jerusalem, for I have seen your wickedness! My father read many things about Jerusalem — that it would be destroyed and its people would perish by the sword or be carried away captive into Babylon.

After my father had read and seen many great and marvelous things, he cried out to the Lord, saying: Great and marvelous are your works, O Lord God Almighty! Your throne is high in the heavens, and your power, goodness, and mercy extend over all the people of the earth. Because you are merciful, you will not allow those who come to you to perish!
This is how my father praised his God, for his soul rejoiced and his whole heart was filled because of the things the Lord had shown him.

Now I, Nephi, am not writing a complete account of everything my father wrote, because he wrote many things about his visions, dreams, prophecies, and the things he taught his children. I will not include all of them.

But I will write about my own experiences. I am making a summary of my father's record on plates that I made with my own hands. After I have summarized my father's record, I will write about my own life.

I want you to know that after the Lord had shown my father, Lehi, so many marvelous things about the destruction of Jerusalem, he went out among the people and began to prophesy and tell them about the things he had seen and heard.

The Jews mocked him because of the things he told them. He truly testified of their wickedness and their sins. He testified that the things he saw, heard, and read in the book clearly showed the coming of a Messiah and the redemption of the world.

When the Jews heard these things, they were angry with him, just as they had been with the prophets before him, whom they had cast out, stoned, and killed. They also tried to take his life. But I, Nephi, will show you that the tender mercies of the Lord are over all those he has chosen because of their faith, to make them mighty even to the power of deliverance.

2.text: The Lord spoke to my father in a dream, saying: You are blessed, Lehi, because of what you have done. Because you have been faithful and declared to this people the things I commanded you, they now seek to take your life.
And the Lord commanded my father in a dream to take his family and leave into the wilderness.
And he obeyed the word of the Lord and did as the Lord commanded him.

He departed into the wilderness. He left his house, his land, his gold, his silver, and his valuable possessions, and took nothing with him except his family, provisions, and tents, and headed into the wilderness.

He traveled down near the shores of the Red Sea, journeying through the wilderness along the borders closer to the Red Sea, with his family — my mother, Sariah, and my older brothers, Laman, Lemuel, and Sam.
After he had traveled three days in the wilderness, he set up his tent in a valley beside a river.
He built an altar of stones, made an offering to the Lord, and gave thanks to the Lord our God.
He named the river Laman. It flowed into the Red Sea, and the valley was near where the river met the sea.
When my father saw that the river flowed into the Red Sea, he said to Laman: I wish you could be like this river, always flowing toward the source of all righteousness!
He also said to Lemuel: I wish you could be like this valley — firm, steadfast, and unmovable in keeping the commandments of the Lord!

He said this because of the stubbornness of Laman and Lemuel. They complained about many things against their father because he was a man of visions and had led them out of Jerusalem, leaving behind their land, gold, silver, and valuables to die in the wilderness. They said he had done this because of the foolish ideas in his heart.
And so Laman and Lemuel, being the oldest, complained against their father. They complained because they did not understand the ways of the God who had created them.
They did not believe that Jerusalem, that great city, could be destroyed as the prophets had said. They were like the Jews in Jerusalem who had tried to take my father's life.

My father spoke to them in the valley of Lemuel with power, being filled with the Spirit, until they shook before him. He silenced them so completely that they did not dare speak against him, and they did as he commanded.
And my father lived in a tent.

I, Nephi, was very young but large in size, and I had a strong desire to know the mysteries of God. So I prayed to the Lord, and he visited me and softened my heart so that I believed all the words my father had spoken. Because of this, I did not rebel against him like my brothers did.
I told Sam about the things the Lord had revealed to me through his Holy Spirit, and Sam believed my words.
But Laman and Lemuel would not listen to my words. I was troubled by the hardness of their hearts, so I prayed to the Lord for them.
And the Lord said to me: You are blessed, Nephi, because of your faith, for you have searched for me with sincerity and humility.
As long as you keep my commandments, you will prosper and be led to a land of promise — a land I have prepared for you, a land that is better than all other lands.
If your brothers rebel against you, they will be cut off from the presence of the Lord.
As long as you keep my commandments, you will be made a ruler and a teacher over your brothers.
In the day that they rebel against me, I will curse them with a terrible curse, and they will have no power over your descendants unless your descendants also rebel against me.
And if your descendants rebel against me, your brothers' descendants will be a punishment to them, to remind them to follow my ways.

3.text: After speaking with the Lord, I, Nephi, returned to the tent of my father.
He said to me: I have had a dream in which the Lord commanded that you and your brothers must return to Jerusalem.
Laban has the record of the Jews and a genealogy of my ancestors, and they are engraved on plates of brass.
The Lord has commanded that you and your brothers go to the house of Laban, get the records, and bring them here into the wilderness.
Now, your brothers are complaining, saying that what I have asked of them is too hard. But I have not asked this of them — it is a commandment of the Lord.
So go, my son, and you will be blessed by the Lord because you have not complained.

And I, Nephi, said to my father: I will go and do the things which the Lord hath commanded, for I know that the Lord gives no commandments to the children of men unless he prepares a way for them to accomplish what he has commanded.
When my father heard these words, he was overjoyed because he knew that I had been blessed by the Lord.
So I, Nephi, and my brothers set out on our journey through the wilderness, with our tents, heading up to the land of Jerusalem.
When we arrived in the land of Jerusalem, my brothers and I discussed what to do.
We cast lots to decide who should go into the house of Laban. The lot fell on Laman, so Laman went into Laban's house and spoke with him.
He asked Laban for the records engraved on the plates of brass, which contained the genealogy of my father.
Laban became angry and threw him out, refusing to give him the records. He said to him: You are a robber, and I will kill you.
Laman fled from Laban's presence and told us what had happened. We became very upset, and my brothers wanted to return to our father in the wilderness.
But I said to them: As the Lord lives, and as we live, we will not go back to our father in the wilderness until we have accomplished what the Lord has commanded us.

So let us be faithful in keeping the commandments of the Lord. Let us go down to the land of our father's inheritance, because he left behind gold, silver, and all kinds of riches — and he did all this because of the commandments of the Lord.
He knew that Jerusalem had to be destroyed because of the wickedness of the people.
They had rejected the words of the prophets. If my father had stayed in the land after being commanded to leave, he would have also perished. So he had to flee.
And it is wise that we should obtain these records so we can preserve the language of our fathers for our children.
We also need to preserve for them the words spoken by all the holy prophets, which were given to them by the Spirit and power of God from the beginning of the world to the present time.
In this way, I persuaded my brothers to be faithful in keeping the commandments of God.
So we went down to the land of our inheritance and gathered together our gold, silver, and valuable things.
After we had gathered these things, we went again to the house of Laban.
We went to Laban and asked him to give us the records engraved on the plates of brass in exchange for our gold, silver, and all our valuables.
When Laban saw our property and how valuable it was, he wanted it for himself. He threw us out and sent his servants to kill us so he could take our property.
We fled from Laban's servants and had to leave behind our property, which fell into Laban's hands.
We escaped into the wilderness. Laban's servants did not catch us, and we hid in the opening of a rock.
Laman was angry with me and with my father, and so was Lemuel, because he listened to Laman. Laman and Lemuel spoke many harsh words to us, their younger brothers, and they even hit us with a rod.

As they were hitting us with a rod, an angel of the Lord appeared and stood before them, saying: Why are you hitting your younger brother with a rod? The Lord has chosen him to be a ruler over you because of your sins. You will go up to Jerusalem again, and the Lord will deliver Laban into your hands.
After the angel had spoken to us, he left.

After the angel left, Laman and Lemuel began complaining again, saying: How can the Lord possibly deliver Laban into our hands? He is a powerful man who can command fifty men — he could easily kill us.

4.text: I said to my brothers: Let us go up to Jerusalem again and be faithful in keeping the commandments of the Lord. He is mightier than all the earth — so why not mightier than Laban and his fifty men, or even his tens of thousands?

So let us go. Let us be strong like Moses, for he truly spoke to the waters of the Red Sea and they parted, and our ancestors came through on dry ground while Pharaoh's armies followed and were drowned.

You know this is true, and you also know that an angel has spoken to you. So why do you doubt? Let us go — the Lord is able to deliver us just as he delivered our ancestors, and to defeat Laban just as he defeated the Egyptians.
After I said these words, they were still angry and kept complaining. But they followed me until we came outside the walls of Jerusalem.
It was nighttime. I had my brothers hide outside the walls. After they hid, I, Nephi, crept into the city and headed toward the house of Laban.
I was led by the Spirit, not knowing ahead of time what I would do.
I went forward, and as I came near the house of Laban, I saw a man lying on the ground in front of me. He was drunk with wine.
When I came closer, I found that it was Laban.
I saw his sword and drew it from its sheath. The handle was made of pure gold, and the craftsmanship was extremely fine. The blade was made of the finest steel.
The Spirit pressed upon me that I should kill Laban, but I said in my heart: I have never taken a man's life. And I held back, not wanting to kill him.

The Spirit said to me again: The Lord has delivered him into your hands. I also knew that he had tried to take my life, that he would not obey the commandments of the Lord, and that he had stolen our property.
The Spirit said to me again: Kill him, for the Lord has delivered him into your hands.
The Lord allows the wicked to be destroyed to bring about his righteous purposes. It is better that one man should die than that an entire nation should fade away and perish in unbelief.
When I, Nephi, heard these words, I remembered what the Lord had told me in the wilderness: As long as your descendants keep my commandments, they will prosper in the land of promise.
I also realized that they could not keep the commandments of the Lord according to the law of Moses unless they had the law.
And I knew that the law was engraved on the plates of brass.
Again, I knew that the Lord had delivered Laban into my hands for this very reason — so I could obtain the records according to his commandments.
So I obeyed the voice of the Spirit, took Laban by the hair of the head, and cut off his head with his own sword.
After I had cut off his head with his own sword, I took Laban's clothes and put them on myself, every piece, and strapped on his armor around my waist.
After doing this, I went to Laban's treasury. As I approached, I saw Laban's servant who had the keys to the treasury. I commanded him in Laban's voice to come with me into the treasury.
He thought I was his master, Laban, because he saw the clothes and the sword strapped around my waist.
He talked to me about the elders of the Jews, knowing that his master, Laban, had been out among them that night.
I spoke to him as if I were Laban.
I also told him that I needed to carry the engravings on the plates of brass out to my older brothers, who were outside the walls.
I also told him to follow me.
He assumed I was talking about members of the church and that I was truly the Laban I had killed, so he followed me.
He talked to me many times about the elders of the Jews as I went out to my brothers, who were outside the walls.
When Laman saw me, he was terrified, and so were Lemuel and Sam. They ran from me because they thought I was Laban and that he had killed me and was coming to take their lives too.
I called out to them, and they heard me and stopped running.
When Laban's servant saw my brothers, he began to tremble and was about to run back to the city of Jerusalem.
Now I, Nephi, being large in size and having received great strength from the Lord, grabbed Laban's servant and held him so he could not escape.
I told him that if he would listen to my words — as the Lord lives and as I live — if he would listen to us, we would spare his life.
I told him with an oath that he had no reason to be afraid — that he would be a free man like us if he came with us into the wilderness.

I also said to him: Surely the Lord has commanded us to do this. Should we not be diligent in keeping the Lord's commandments? If you will come with us into the wilderness to my father, you will have a place with us.
Zoram took courage from my words. (Zoram was the name of the servant.) He promised to go into the wilderness with us to our father, and he made an oath to stay with us from that time on.
We wanted him to stay with us so the Jews would not find out about our escape into the wilderness and come after us to destroy us.
When Zoram had made his oath to us, our fears about him were gone.
We took the plates of brass and Laban's servant and left for the wilderness, traveling to the tent of our father.

5.text: After we came down into the wilderness to our father, he was filled with joy. My mother, Sariah, was also overjoyed, because she had truly been mourning over us.

She had thought we had died in the wilderness. She had also complained against my father, calling him a man of visions and saying: You have led us out of the land of our inheritance, and my sons are gone, and we will die in the wilderness.
This is how my mother had complained against my father.

My father said to her: I know that I am a man of visions. If I had not seen the things of God in a vision, I would not have known the goodness of God, and I would have stayed in Jerusalem and died with my people.
But I have obtained a land of promise, and I rejoice in that. I know that the Lord will rescue my sons from Laban and bring them back to us in the wilderness.
In this way, my father, Lehi, comforted my mother, Sariah, while we were journeying through the wilderness up to Jerusalem to get the record of the Jews.
When we returned to the tent of my father, their joy was complete, and my mother was comforted.

She said: Now I know for certain that the Lord commanded my husband to flee into the wilderness. I also know for certain that the Lord has protected my sons and delivered them from Laban, and given them power to accomplish what the Lord commanded them. This is what she said.
They rejoiced greatly, offered sacrifices and burnt offerings to the Lord, and gave thanks to the God of Israel.
After giving thanks to the God of Israel, my father, Lehi, took the records engraved on the plates of brass and examined them from the beginning.
He found that they contained the five books of Moses, which gave an account of the creation of the world and of Adam and Eve, our first parents.
They also contained a history of the Jews from the beginning down to the start of the reign of Zedekiah, king of Judah.
They also contained the prophecies of the holy prophets from the beginning down to the start of Zedekiah's reign, including many prophecies spoken by Jeremiah.

My father, Lehi, also found a genealogy of his ancestors on the plates of brass. He learned that he was a descendant of Joseph — the Joseph who was the son of Jacob, who was sold into Egypt and was preserved by the Lord so he could save his father, Jacob, and his entire family from dying of famine.
They were also led out of captivity and out of Egypt by that same God who had preserved them.
In this way, my father, Lehi, discovered the genealogy of his ancestors. Laban was also a descendant of Joseph, which is why he and his family had kept the records.
When my father saw all these things, he was filled with the Spirit and began to prophesy about his descendants.
He prophesied that these plates of brass would go out to all nations, families, languages, and people who descended from him.
He said that these plates of brass would never be destroyed and would never fade with time. And he prophesied many things about his descendants.
Up to this point, my father and I had kept the commandments the Lord had given us.

We had obtained the records the Lord commanded us to get, and we searched them and found they were valuable — of great worth to us — because through them we could preserve the Lord's commandments for our children.
So it was wise that the Lord had us carry them with us as we traveled through the wilderness toward the land of promise.

6.text: Now I, Nephi, am not including the genealogy of my ancestors in this part of my record, nor will I include it later on these plates, because it is already recorded in the record kept by my father. So I will not write it here.
It is enough for me to say that we are descendants of Joseph.
It does not matter to me to give a complete account of everything about my father, because there is not room for it on these plates. I want to use the space to write about the things of God.
My whole purpose is to persuade people to come to the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, and be saved.
So I do not write things that please the world, but things that please God and those who are not focused on the world.
Therefore, I will command my descendants not to fill these plates with things that have no value to the children of men.

7.text: After my father, Lehi, had finished prophesying about his descendants, the Lord spoke to him again, saying it was not right for Lehi to take his family into the wilderness alone. His sons needed to marry so they could have children for the Lord in the land of promise.
The Lord commanded him that I, Nephi, and my brothers should return to the land of Jerusalem and bring Ishmael and his family into the wilderness.
So I, Nephi, and my brothers set out again through the wilderness to go up to Jerusalem.
We went to the house of Ishmael and gained his trust, and we told him the words of the Lord.
The Lord softened the heart of Ishmael and his household, and they traveled with us down into the wilderness to the tent of our father.

As we traveled through the wilderness, Laman, Lemuel, two of the daughters of Ishmael, and the two sons of Ishmael and their families rebelled against us — against me, Nephi, and Sam, and against their father, Ishmael, his wife, and his three other daughters.
In their rebellion, they wanted to return to the land of Jerusalem.

I, Nephi, was troubled by the hardness of their hearts, so I spoke to them — to Laman and Lemuel — saying: You are my older brothers. How is it that you are so stubborn and so blind that you need me, your younger brother, to speak to you and set an example for you?
How is it that you have not listened to the word of the Lord?
How is it that you have forgotten that you saw an angel of the Lord?
How is it that you have forgotten the great things the Lord has done for us in rescuing us from Laban and helping us obtain the record?
How is it that you have forgotten that the Lord can do all things according to his will for the children of men, if they have faith in him? So let us be faithful to him.

If we are faithful to him, we will receive the land of promise. And you will know in the future that the Lord's word about the destruction of Jerusalem will come true, because everything the Lord has said about Jerusalem's destruction must be fulfilled.

The Spirit of the Lord will soon stop working with them, because they have rejected the prophets. They have thrown Jeremiah into prison. They have tried to kill my father and have driven him out of the land.

I say to you that if you go back to Jerusalem, you will die with them. If you have a choice, go back to the land, but remember what I am telling you — if you go, you will also perish. The Spirit of the Lord compels me to say this.

When I, Nephi, had said these things to my brothers, they were furious. They grabbed me, for they were extremely angry, and they tied me up with ropes, planning to leave me in the wilderness to be eaten by wild animals.
But I prayed to the Lord, saying: O Lord, according to my faith in you, please deliver me from the hands of my brothers. Give me the strength to break these ropes that bind me.
After I said these words, the ropes were loosened from my hands and feet, and I stood before my brothers and spoke to them again.

They were angry with me again and tried to grab me. But one of the daughters of Ishmael, along with her mother and one of the sons of Ishmael, pleaded with my brothers until they softened their hearts and stopped trying to take my life.
They felt sorry because of their wickedness, and they bowed down before me and begged me to forgive them for what they had done.
I freely forgave them everything and urged them to pray to the Lord their God for forgiveness. And they did. After they finished praying, we continued our journey toward the tent of our father.

We arrived at the tent of our father. And after I and my brothers and all the house of Ishmael had arrived at the tent of my father, they gave thanks to the Lord their God and offered sacrifices and burnt offerings to him.

8.text: We had gathered together seeds of every kind — every type of grain and every type of fruit.
While my father was staying in the wilderness, he spoke to us, saying: I have had a dream — or in other words, I have seen a vision.
Because of what I have seen, I have reason to rejoice in the Lord because of Nephi and Sam, for I have reason to believe that they and many of their descendants will be saved.
But Laman and Lemuel, I am deeply worried about you, because in my dream I saw a dark and dreary wilderness.
I saw a man dressed in a white robe, and he came and stood before me.
He spoke to me and told me to follow him.
As I followed him, I found myself in a dark and dreary wasteland.
After I had traveled for many hours in darkness, I began to pray to the Lord to have mercy on me, because of his many tender mercies.
After I prayed to the Lord, I saw a large and open field.
I saw a tree whose fruit was able to make a person happy.
I went to the tree and ate its fruit. It was the sweetest thing I had ever tasted. The fruit was white, whiter than anything I had ever seen.
As I ate the fruit, my soul was filled with tremendous joy. I wanted my family to eat it too, because I knew it was the most desirable of all fruit.
As I looked around, hoping to find my family, I saw a river of water running near the tree where I was eating the fruit.
I looked to see where the river began, and a short distance away at its source I saw your mother Sariah, and Sam, and Nephi. They stood there as if they did not know where to go.
I waved to them and called out with a loud voice for them to come to me and eat the fruit, which was more desirable than any other fruit.
They came to me and also ate the fruit.
I wanted Laman and Lemuel to come and eat the fruit too, so I looked toward the source of the river, hoping to see them.
I saw them, but they would not come to me and eat the fruit.
I saw a rod of iron that extended along the bank of the river and led to the tree where I stood.

I also saw a strait and narrow path that ran alongside the rod of iron, leading to the tree where I stood. It also went past the source of the river and out to a large and spacious field, as if it were a whole world.
I saw countless crowds of people, many of whom were pushing forward to reach the path that led to the tree.
They came and started walking on the path that led to the tree.
Then a mist of darkness arose — an extremely thick mist of darkness — so that those who had started on the path lost their way, wandered off, and were lost.

I saw others pressing forward who came and grabbed hold of the end of the rod of iron. They pressed forward through the mist of darkness, clinging to the rod of iron, until they reached the tree and ate its fruit.
After they had eaten the fruit of the tree, they looked around as if they were ashamed.
I also looked around and saw, on the other side of the river, a great and spacious building. It seemed to stand in the air, high above the earth.
It was filled with people — old and young, men and women. They were dressed in very fine clothing, and they were mocking and pointing their fingers at those who had come and were eating the fruit.
After they had tasted the fruit, they were ashamed because of those mocking them, and they wandered away into forbidden paths and were lost.
Now I, Nephi, am not including all the words of my father.

To be brief, my father saw other crowds pressing forward. They came and grabbed hold of the end of the rod of iron and pressed forward, continually holding tight to the rod of iron, until they reached the tree and fell down and ate its fruit.
He also saw other crowds feeling their way toward the great and spacious building.
Many were drowned in the depths of the river, and many wandered off onto strange roads and were lost from his view.
A great number entered the strange building. After they entered, they pointed the finger of scorn at me and at those who were also eating the fruit. But we paid no attention to them.
These are the words of my father: As many as paid attention to the mockers fell away.
Laman and Lemuel did not eat the fruit, said my father.

After my father had told us everything about his dream or vision, which included many things, he said that because of what he had seen, he was deeply afraid for Laman and Lemuel — afraid that they would be cast away from the presence of the Lord.
He pleaded with them with all the feeling of a loving parent, urging them to listen to his words so the Lord might be merciful and not cast them away. My father truly preached to them.
After he had preached to them and prophesied many things, he told them to keep the commandments of the Lord. Then he finished speaking to them.

9.text: My father saw, heard, and spoke about all these things while living in a tent in the valley of Lemuel, along with many other things that cannot be written on these plates.

Now, regarding these plates — they are not the plates on which I am writing the full history of my people. I have named those other plates the plates of Nephi, after my own name. These plates are also called the plates of Nephi.
I received a commandment from the Lord to make these plates for the specific purpose of recording an account of the ministry of my people.

The other plates contain an account of the kings, wars, and conflicts of my people. So these plates are mostly about the ministry, and the other plates are mostly about the kings, wars, and conflicts.
The Lord commanded me to make these plates for a wise purpose known to him, which I do not yet understand.
But the Lord knows all things from the beginning, and he prepares a way to accomplish all his work among the children of men, for he has all power to fulfill all his words. And so it is. Amen.

10.text: Now I, Nephi, continue my account on these plates of my experiences, my leadership, and my ministry. To do this, I must first say more about my father and my brothers.
After my father finished telling about his dream and encouraging them to be diligent, he spoke to them about the Jews.

He told them that after the great city of Jerusalem was destroyed and many people were carried away captive into Babylon, in the Lord's own time they would return — they would be brought back from captivity and would possess the land of their inheritance again.
Six hundred years after my father left Jerusalem, the Lord God would raise up a prophet among the Jews — a Messiah, or in other words, a Savior of the world.
He also spoke about the prophets and how many of them had testified about this Messiah, this Redeemer of the world.
Therefore, all mankind were in a lost and fallen state, and would be forever unless they relied on this Redeemer.
He also spoke of a prophet who would come before the Messiah to prepare the way of the Lord.

This prophet would go forth and cry in the wilderness: Prepare the way of the Lord and make his paths straight, for there stands one among you whom you do not know. He is mightier than I, and I am not worthy to untie his shoes. My father spoke much about this.
My father said this prophet would baptize in Bethabara, beyond the Jordan River. He said he would baptize with water — that he would baptize the Messiah with water.
After baptizing the Messiah with water, he would see and testify that he had baptized the Lamb of God, who would take away the sins of the world.

After my father spoke these words, he told my brothers about the gospel that would be preached among the Jews and about the Jews falling into unbelief. After they had killed the Messiah who was to come, after he had been killed he would rise from the dead and would reveal himself through the Holy Ghost to the Gentiles.
My father spoke much about the Gentiles and about the house of Israel, saying they would be compared to an olive tree whose branches would be broken off and scattered across the whole earth.
He said that we had to be led together into the land of promise, fulfilling the Lord's word that we would be scattered across the earth.

After the house of Israel was scattered, they would be gathered together again. In short, after the Gentiles had received the fulness of the Gospel, the natural branches of the olive tree — the remnants of the house of Israel — would be grafted back in, coming to know the true Messiah, their Lord and Redeemer.
In this way, my father prophesied and spoke to my brothers, along with many other things that I have not written in this book. I have written as many of them as was useful in my other book.
All these things happened while my father lived in a tent in the valley of Lemuel.

After I, Nephi, heard all the words of my father about the things he saw in his vision and what he spoke by the power of the Holy Ghost — which power he received through faith in the Son of God, the Messiah who was to come — I, Nephi, also wanted to see, hear, and know these things by the power of the Holy Ghost, which is the gift of God to all who sincerely seek him, both in ancient times and in the time he would show himself to the children of men.
For he is the same yesterday, today, and forever, and the way has been prepared for all people from the foundation of the world, if they will repent and come to him.

Those who seek sincerely will find, and the mysteries of God will be revealed to them by the power of the Holy Ghost, in these times as well as in ancient times, and in ancient times as well as in times to come. The path of the Lord is one eternal round.
Therefore remember, every person, that you will be brought to judgment for everything you do.
If you have chosen to do wickedly during your time of testing, then you are found unclean before the judgment seat of God. No unclean thing can live with God, so you must be cast off forever.
The Holy Ghost gives me authority to say these things and not hold them back.

11.text: After I had desired to know the things my father had seen, and believing that the Lord could make them known to me, as I sat thinking deeply I was caught away in the Spirit of the Lord to an extremely high mountain that I had never seen before and had never set foot on.
The Spirit said to me: What do you want?
I said: I want to see the things my father saw.
The Spirit said to me: Do you believe that your father saw the tree he spoke about?
I said: Yes, you know that I believe all the words of my father.

When I said this, the Spirit cried out with a loud voice: Hosanna to the Lord, the Most High God, for he is God over all the earth, above all things. And you are blessed, Nephi, because you believe in the Son of the Most High God. Therefore, you will see the things you have desired.

And this will be given to you as a sign: after you have seen the tree that bore the fruit your father tasted, you will also see a man descending from heaven. You will be a witness of him, and after you have witnessed him, you will testify that he is the Son of God.

The Spirit said to me: Look! I looked and saw a tree, and it was like the tree my father had seen. Its beauty was far beyond all other beauty, and its whiteness exceeded the whiteness of freshly fallen snow.
After I saw the tree, I said to the Spirit: I see that you have shown me the tree that is precious above all things.
He said to me: What do you want?

I said to him: I want to know what it means — for I spoke to him as one person speaks to another, because I saw that he was in the form of a man. Yet I knew he was the Spirit of the Lord, and he spoke to me as one person speaks to another.
He said to me: Look! I looked toward him, but I could not see him, for he had gone from my presence.
I looked and saw the great city of Jerusalem and other cities. I saw the city of Nazareth, and in Nazareth I saw a young woman who was extremely beautiful.
I saw the heavens open, and an angel came down and stood before me. He said to me: Nephi, what do you see?
I said to him: A young woman, the most beautiful above all other young women.
He said to me: Do you understand the condescension of God?
I said to him: I know that he loves his children, but I do not know the meaning of all things.
He said to me: The virgin you see is the mother of the Son of God, after the manner of the flesh.
I saw that she was carried away in the Spirit. After she had been carried away in the Spirit for a time, the angel said to me: Look!
I looked and saw the virgin again, this time holding a child in her arms.
The angel said to me: Behold the Lamb of God, the Son of the Eternal Father! Do you know the meaning of the tree your father saw?
I answered him: Yes, it is the love of God, which spreads throughout the hearts of the children of men. Therefore, it is the most desirable of all things.
He said to me: Yes, and the most joyful to the soul.
After he said this, he said to me: Look! I looked and saw the Son of God going among the children of men, and I saw many people fall at his feet and worship him.

I saw that the rod of iron my father had seen was the word of God, which led to the fountain of living waters, or to the tree of life. These waters represent the love of God, and I also saw that the tree of life was a representation of the love of God.
The angel said to me again: Look and behold the condescension of God!

I looked and saw the Redeemer of the world, whom my father had spoken of. I also saw the prophet who would prepare the way before him. The Lamb of God went and was baptized by him. After he was baptized, I saw the heavens open and the Holy Ghost come down from heaven and rest upon him in the form of a dove.
I saw that he went forth serving the people with power and great glory. Crowds gathered to hear him, and I saw that they rejected him.
I also saw twelve others following him. They were carried away in the Spirit and disappeared from my sight.
The angel spoke to me again, saying: Look! I looked and saw the heavens open again, and I saw angels coming down to the children of men and serving them.

He spoke to me again, saying: Look! I looked and saw the Lamb of God going among the children of men. I saw crowds of people who were sick and afflicted with all kinds of diseases, and tormented by devils and unclean spirits. The angel showed all these things to me. They were healed by the power of the Lamb of God, and the devils and unclean spirits were cast out.
The angel spoke to me again, saying: Look! I looked and saw the Lamb of God taken by the people. The Son of the everlasting God was judged by the world, and I saw it and testify of it.
And I, Nephi, saw that he was lifted up on the cross and killed for the sins of the world.
After he was killed, I saw the multitudes of the earth gathered together to fight against the apostles of the Lamb, for that is what the angel of the Lord called the twelve.

The multitude of the earth gathered together, and I saw that they were in a large and spacious building like the one my father saw. The angel of the Lord said to me: See the world and its wisdom. The house of Israel has gathered together to fight against the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

I saw and testify that the great and spacious building represented the pride of the world, and it fell — and its fall was tremendously great. The angel of the Lord said to me: This is how all nations, families, languages, and peoples who fight against the twelve apostles of the Lamb will be destroyed.

12.text: The angel said to me, "Look and see your descendants, and also the descendants of your brothers." I looked and saw the promised land, and I saw huge crowds of people, as many as the sand of the sea.
I saw large groups gathered together to fight against each other, and I saw wars and rumors of wars and terrible slaughter by the sword among my people.
I saw many generations pass away, filled with wars and conflicts in the land. I saw many cities—so many that I could not count them.

I saw a mist of darkness covering the promised land. I saw lightning and heard thunder and earthquakes and all kinds of terrible noises. I saw the earth and rocks split apart. I saw mountains crumble to pieces. I saw the flat lands break up. I saw many cities sink into the ground, many burned with fire, and many collapse because of the shaking.
After I saw these things, I saw the cloud of darkness pass away from the earth. And I saw crowds of people who had survived the great and terrible judgments of the Lord.
I saw the heavens open and the Lamb of God coming down from heaven. He came down and showed himself to the people.
I also saw and testify that the Holy Ghost came upon twelve others, and they were ordained and chosen by God.
The angel said to me, "Look at the twelve disciples of the Lamb, who are chosen to serve your descendants."

He said to me, "Do you remember the twelve apostles of the Lamb? They are the ones who will judge the twelve tribes of Israel. Therefore, the twelve ministers of your descendants will be judged by them, because you are part of the house of Israel."
"These twelve ministers that you see will judge your descendants. And they are righteous forever, because their faith in the Lamb of God has made their garments white through his blood."

The angel said to me, "Look!" I looked and saw three generations pass away in righteousness, and their garments were white like those of the Lamb of God. The angel said to me, "These are made white through the blood of the Lamb, because of their faith in him."
I, Nephi, also saw many people of the fourth generation who passed away in righteousness.
I saw the multitudes of the earth gathered together.
The angel said to me, "Look at your descendants, and also the descendants of your brothers."
I looked and saw my descendants gathered together in large groups to fight against the descendants of my brothers, and they were assembled for battle.
The angel said to me, "Look at the fountain of filthy water that your father saw—the river he spoke of. Its depths represent the depths of hell."
"The mists of darkness represent the temptations of the devil, which blind people's eyes, harden their hearts, and lead them onto wide roads where they are lost and destroyed."

"The large and spacious building that your father saw represents the empty pride and vain imaginations of people. A great and terrible gulf separates them—the word of the justice of the Eternal God and the Messiah, who is the Lamb of God, of whom the Holy Ghost has testified from the beginning of the world until now, and from now on forever."

While the angel spoke these words, I saw that the descendants of my brothers fought against my descendants, just as the angel said. Because of the pride of my descendants and the temptations of the devil, I saw that the descendants of my brothers overpowered my people.
I saw that the descendants of my brothers had overcome my descendants, and they spread out in great numbers across the land.
I saw them gathered in large groups, and I saw wars and rumors of wars among them. Through wars and rumors of wars, I saw many generations pass away.
The angel said to me, "These people will lose their faith and fall into unbelief."
I saw that after they had fallen into unbelief, they became a dark, disgusting, and filthy people, full of laziness and every kind of wickedness.

13.text: The angel said to me, "Look!" And I looked and saw many nations and kingdoms.
The angel asked me, "What do you see?" I said, "I see many nations and kingdoms."
He said to me, "These are the nations and kingdoms of the Gentiles."
I saw among the Gentile nations the formation of a great church.

The angel said to me, "Look at the formation of a church that is the most wicked of all churches. It kills the saints of God, tortures them, ties them down, puts a yoke of iron on them, and brings them into captivity."
I saw this great and wicked church, and I saw that the devil was its founder.
I also saw gold, silver, silks, scarlet cloth, fine linen, and all kinds of expensive clothing, and I saw many immoral people.
The angel said to me, "The gold, silver, silks, scarlet cloth, fine linen, expensive clothing, and the immoral people are the desires of this great and wicked church."
"They also destroy the saints of God and bring them into captivity to gain the praise of the world."
I looked and saw many waters that separated the Gentiles from the descendants of my brothers.
The angel said to me, "The wrath of God is upon the descendants of your brothers."

I looked and saw a man among the Gentiles who was separated from the descendants of my brothers by the great waters. I saw the Spirit of God come down and inspire him, and he traveled across the great waters to the descendants of my brothers, who were in the promised land.
I saw the Spirit of God inspire other Gentiles, and they came out of captivity and traveled across the great waters.
I saw large numbers of Gentiles in the promised land. I saw that the wrath of God was upon the descendants of my brothers, and they were scattered and defeated by the Gentiles.

I saw that the Spirit of the Lord was with the Gentiles, and they prospered and obtained the land as their inheritance. I saw that they were white and very fair and beautiful, like my people had been before they were killed.
I, Nephi, saw that the Gentiles who had come out of captivity humbled themselves before the Lord, and the power of the Lord was with them.
I saw that the mother nations of the Gentiles gathered on the waters and on the land to fight against them.
I saw that the power of God was with the colonists, and the wrath of God was upon all who gathered to fight against them.
I, Nephi, saw that the Gentiles who had come out of captivity were delivered by the power of God from all other nations.
I, Nephi, saw that they prospered in the land, and I saw a book being carried among them.
The angel asked me, "Do you know the meaning of the book?"
I said to him, "I do not know."

He said, "It comes from the mouth of a Jew." I, Nephi, saw it, and he said to me, "The book you see is a record of the Jews. It contains the covenants the Lord made with the house of Israel, and it also contains many prophecies of the holy prophets. It is a record similar to the engravings on the plates of brass, though not as extensive. Nevertheless, it contains the covenants of the Lord made with the house of Israel, and therefore it is of great worth to the Gentiles."

The angel of the Lord said to me, "You have seen that the book came from a Jew. When it came from the Jew, it contained the fulness of the gospel of the Lord, to which the twelve apostles testify. They testify according to the truth that is in the Lamb of God."
"Therefore, these things go from the Jews to the Gentiles in purity, according to the truth that is in God."

"After the twelve apostles of the Lamb pass these things from the Jews to the Gentiles, you see the formation of that great and wicked church, which is the most evil of all churches. They have removed many plain and extremely precious parts from the gospel of the Lamb, and they have also taken away many covenants of the Lord."
"They have done all this to twist the right ways of the Lord, to blind people's eyes, and to harden their hearts."
"So you see that after the book has passed through the hands of the great and wicked church, many plain and precious things have been removed from it—the book of the Lamb of God."

"After these plain and precious things were removed, the book goes to all the Gentile nations. After it goes to all the Gentile nations—even across the great waters you have seen, with the Gentiles who came out of captivity—because of the many plain and precious things taken from the book that were easy for people to understand, according to the plainness of the Lamb of God—because of what was removed from the gospel of the Lamb, a very great many people stumble, so much that Satan has great power over them."

"Nevertheless, you see that the Gentiles who came out of captivity and were lifted up by the power of God above all other nations, upon the land that is the most choice of all lands—the land that the Lord God promised your father Abraham that his descendants would inherit—you see that the Lord God will not allow the Gentiles to completely destroy the mixed remnant of your descendants who are among your brothers."
"Neither will he allow the Gentiles to destroy the descendants of your brothers."

"Neither will the Lord God allow the Gentiles to stay forever in the terrible state of blindness they are in because of the plain and most precious parts of the gospel of the Lamb that have been held back by that wicked church whose formation you have seen."
"Therefore the Lamb of God says, 'I will be merciful to the Gentiles, as I visit the remnant of the house of Israel in great judgment.'"

The angel of the Lord said to me, "The Lamb of God says, 'After I have visited the remnant of the house of Israel—this remnant I speak of is the descendants of your father—after I have visited them in judgment and struck them through the Gentiles, and after the Gentiles stumble greatly because of the most plain and precious parts of the gospel of the Lamb held back by that wicked church, the mother of harlots—I will be merciful to the Gentiles in that day. I will bring forth to them, by my own power, much of my gospel, which will be plain and precious,' says the Lamb."

"'For,' says the Lamb, 'I will reveal myself to your descendants, and they will write many things that I will teach them, which will be plain and precious. After your descendants are destroyed and fall into unbelief, along with the descendants of your brothers, these things will be hidden and then brought to the Gentiles by the gift and power of the Lamb.'"
"'And in them will be written my gospel,' says the Lamb, 'and my rock and my salvation.'"

"'Blessed are those who seek to build up my Zion in that day, for they will have the gift and power of the Holy Ghost. If they endure to the end, they will be lifted up at the last day and saved in the everlasting kingdom of the Lamb. And whoever publishes peace—good news of great joy—how beautiful upon the mountains they will be.'"
I saw the remnant of the descendants of my brothers, and also the book of the Lamb of God that came from the Jew—it came from the Gentiles to the remnant of the descendants of my brothers.

After it came to them, I saw other books come forth by the power of the Lamb, from the Gentiles to them, to convince both the Gentiles and the remnant of the descendants of my brothers, and also the Jews scattered across the earth, that the records of the prophets and of the twelve apostles of the Lamb are true.

The angel said to me, "These later records that you have seen among the Gentiles will confirm the truth of the first records from the twelve apostles of the Lamb. They will make known the plain and precious things that were removed, and will make known to all peoples, languages, and nations that the Lamb of God is the Son of the Eternal Father and the Savior of the world, and that all people must come to him or they cannot be saved."

"They must come according to the words established by the Lamb. The words of the Lamb will be made known in the records of your descendants, as well as in the records of the twelve apostles of the Lamb. Therefore both records will be established as one, for there is one God and one Shepherd over all the earth."

"The time will come when he will reveal himself to all nations, both to the Jews and to the Gentiles. After he has revealed himself to the Jews and to the Gentiles, then he will reveal himself to the Gentiles and to the Jews, and the last will be first, and the first will be last."

14.text: It will happen that if the Gentiles listen to the Lamb of God in the day when he reveals himself to them in word, in power, and in real action, removing their stumbling blocks—

And if they do not harden their hearts against the Lamb of God, they will be counted among the descendants of your father. They will be counted among the house of Israel, and they will be a blessed people upon the promised land forever. They will never again be brought into captivity, and the house of Israel will no longer be confused.

That great pit dug by the great and wicked church, which was founded by the devil and his followers to lead people's souls down to hell—that great pit dug for the destruction of people will be filled by those who dug it, leading to their complete destruction, says the Lamb of God. This is not the destruction of the soul, except that it is cast into that hell which has no end.
For this is according to the captivity of the devil and the justice of God upon all who practice wickedness and abomination before him.

The angel said to me, Nephi, "You have seen that if the Gentiles repent, it will go well for them. You also know about the covenants the Lord made with the house of Israel. And you have heard that whoever does not repent must perish."
"Therefore, woe to the Gentiles if they harden their hearts against the Lamb of God."

"For the time is coming," says the Lamb of God, "when I will do a great and marvelous work among the people—a work that will be everlasting in one of two ways: either it will convince them and lead to peace and eternal life, or it will leave them to the hardness of their hearts and blindness of their minds, leading them into captivity and destruction, both physically and spiritually, according to the captivity of the devil that I have spoken of."
When the angel had spoken these words, he asked me, "Do you remember the covenants of the Father with the house of Israel?" I said to him, "Yes."
He said to me, "Look and see that great and wicked church, the mother of abominations, whose founder is the devil."

He said to me, "There are only two churches: the church of the Lamb of God and the church of the devil. Whoever does not belong to the church of the Lamb of God belongs to that great church, the mother of abominations—the whore of all the earth."
I looked and saw the whore of all the earth sitting upon many waters, and she had control over all the earth, among all nations, peoples, languages, and races.

I saw the church of the Lamb of God, and its members were few because of the wickedness and abominations of the whore who sat upon many waters. Nevertheless, I saw that the church of the Lamb—the saints of God—was spread across all the earth, though their territories were small because of the wickedness of the great whore.
I saw that the great mother of abominations gathered together large crowds from all the nations of the Gentiles across the whole earth to fight against the Lamb of God.

I, Nephi, saw the power of the Lamb of God come down upon the saints of the church of the Lamb and upon the covenant people of the Lord who were scattered across the earth. They were armed with righteousness and with the power of God in great glory.
I saw that the wrath of God was poured out upon the great and wicked church, so much that there were wars and rumors of wars among all the nations and peoples of the earth.
As wars and rumors of wars began among all the nations that belonged to the mother of abominations, the angel said to me, "The wrath of God is upon the mother of harlots. You see all these things—"

"When the day comes that the wrath of God is poured out upon the mother of harlots—the great and wicked church of all the earth whose founder is the devil—then the work of the Father will begin to prepare the way for fulfilling his covenants with his people of the house of Israel."
The angel said to me, "Look!"
I looked and saw a man dressed in a white robe.
The angel said to me, "This is one of the twelve apostles of the Lamb."
"He will see and write the rest of these things, and also many things that have already happened."
"He will also write about the end of the world."

"The things he will write are just and true. They are written in the book that you saw coming from the Jew. When they originally came from the Jew, the things written were plain, pure, extremely precious, and easy for everyone to understand."
"The things this apostle of the Lamb will write include many things you have seen. You will see the rest."
"But the things you see after this you must not write, because the Lord God has appointed the apostle of the Lamb of God to write them."

"Others who have lived before have also been shown all things, and they have written them. Their writings are sealed up to come forth in their purity, according to the truth in the Lamb, at the Lord's own chosen time, to the house of Israel."
I, Nephi, heard and testify that the name of the apostle of the Lamb was John, according to the word of the angel.
I, Nephi, am forbidden to write the rest of the things I saw and heard. What I have already written is enough for me, and I have written only a small part of what I saw.
I testify that I saw the things my father saw, and the angel of the Lord made them known to me.
Now I stop speaking about the things I saw while I was carried away in the Spirit. If not everything I saw is written here, the things I have written are true. And so it is. Amen.

15.text: After I, Nephi, had been carried away in the Spirit and seen all these things, I returned to my father's tent.
I found my brothers arguing with each other about the things our father had told them.

He had truly spoken many great things to them that were hard to understand unless a person asked the Lord. But because they were hard in their hearts, they did not turn to the Lord as they should have.
I, Nephi, was grieved because of the hardness of their hearts, and also because of the things I had seen. I knew these things would surely happen because of the great wickedness of people.
I was overcome by my sorrows, because I considered my grief to be the greatest of all, since I had seen the destruction of my people—I had witnessed their downfall.
After I had regained my strength, I spoke to my brothers, wanting to know what they were arguing about.
They said, "We cannot understand what our father said about the natural branches of the olive tree, or about the Gentiles."
I asked them, "Have you asked the Lord?"
They said to me, "We have not, because the Lord does not make such things known to us."
I said to them, "How is it that you do not keep the commandments of the Lord? How is it that you will perish because of the hardness of your hearts?"

"Do you not remember what the Lord has said? 'If you will not harden your hearts and ask me in faith, believing that you will receive, while diligently keeping my commandments, surely these things will be made known to you.'"
"The house of Israel was compared to an olive tree by the Spirit of the Lord through our father. Are we not broken off from the house of Israel? Are we not a branch of the house of Israel?"

"What our father meant about the grafting in of the natural branches through the fulness of the Gentiles is this: in the latter days, after our descendants have fallen into unbelief for many years and many generations after the Messiah appears in the flesh to the people, then the fulness of the gospel of the Messiah will come to the Gentiles, and from the Gentiles to the remnant of our descendants."

"At that time, the remnant of our descendants will know that they are part of the house of Israel and that they are the Lord's covenant people. Then they will come to know about their ancestors and about the gospel of their Redeemer, which was taught to their fathers by him. They will come to understand their Redeemer and the essential points of his teachings, so they may know how to come to him and be saved."

"Will they not rejoice and praise their everlasting God, their rock and their salvation, on that day? Will they not receive strength and nourishment from the true vine? Will they not come into the true fold of God?"
"I tell you, yes! They will be remembered again among the house of Israel. They will be grafted in as a natural branch of the olive tree, back into the true olive tree."

"This is what our father meant. He meant it will not happen until after they are scattered by the Gentiles, and it will come through the Gentiles, so the Lord can show his power to the Gentiles—precisely because he will be rejected by the Jews, or by the house of Israel."

"Our father was not speaking only about our descendants, but about all the house of Israel, pointing to the covenant that would be fulfilled in the latter days—the covenant the Lord made with our father Abraham, saying, 'Through your descendants, all the families of the earth will be blessed.'"
I, Nephi, spoke much to them about these things, especially about the restoration of the Jews in the latter days.

I shared with them the words of Isaiah, who spoke about the restoration of the Jews, or of the house of Israel—that after they were restored, they would never again be confused or scattered. I spoke many words to my brothers, and they were calmed and humbled themselves before the Lord.
They spoke to me again, saying, "What does our father's dream mean? What does the tree represent?"
I told them it was a representation of the tree of life.
They asked me, "What does the rod of iron that our father saw, which led to the tree, represent?"

I told them it was the word of God, and whoever would listen to the word of God and hold firmly to it would never perish. The temptations and fiery arrows of the adversary could not overpower them with blindness or lead them to destruction.

Therefore, I, Nephi, urged them to pay attention to the word of the Lord. I urged them with all the energy of my soul and every ability I had, to pay attention to the word of God and remember to always keep his commandments in all things.
They asked me, "What does the river of water that our father saw represent?"
I told them the water my father saw represented filthiness, but his mind was so focused on other things that he did not notice the filthiness of the water.
I told them it was a terrible gulf that separated the wicked from the tree of life and from the saints of God.
I told them it represented that awful hell which the angel said was prepared for the wicked.
I told them that our father also saw that the justice of God divides the wicked from the righteous, and its brightness is like a flaming fire that rises up to God forever and ever, without end.
They asked me, "Does this mean the torment of the body during this life, or the final state of the soul after the death of the physical body, or is it about physical things?"

I told them it represented things both physical and spiritual, because the day would come when they would be judged by their works—the works done in their physical bodies during their time of testing.

Therefore, if they die in their wickedness, they must be cast off spiritually as well, in terms of righteousness. They must be brought before God to be judged by their works. If their works have been filthy, then they must be filthy, and if they are filthy, they cannot live in the kingdom of God—otherwise the kingdom of God would also be filthy.
"But I tell you, the kingdom of God is not filthy, and nothing unclean can enter the kingdom of God. Therefore, there must be a place of filthiness prepared for that which is filthy."

"There is a place prepared—that awful hell I have spoken of—and the devil is the one who prepared it. Therefore, the final state of people's souls is either to live in the kingdom of God or to be cast out because of the justice I have spoken about."

"The wicked are separated from the righteous and from the tree of life, whose fruit is the most precious and desirable of all fruits—it is the greatest of all the gifts of God." And this is what I said to my brothers. Amen.

16.text: After I, Nephi, had finished speaking to my brothers, they said to me, "You have told us hard things, more than we can bear."

I told them that I knew I had spoken hard things against the wicked, according to the truth. I had defended the righteous and testified that they would be lifted up at the last day. The guilty think the truth is hard because it cuts them to the very core.

"Now my brothers, if you were righteous and willing to listen to the truth and follow it so you could walk uprightly before God, then you would not complain about the truth or say, 'You speak hard things against us.'"
I, Nephi, urged my brothers with all diligence to keep the commandments of the Lord.
They humbled themselves before the Lord, so much that I had joy and great hope that they would walk in the paths of righteousness.
All these things were said and done while my father was living in a tent in the valley he called Lemuel.
I, Nephi, married one of the daughters of Ishmael. My brothers also married daughters of Ishmael, and Zoram married the eldest daughter of Ishmael.
In this way, my father had fulfilled all the commandments the Lord had given him. And I, Nephi, had been greatly blessed by the Lord.
The voice of the Lord spoke to my father during the night and commanded him to continue his journey into the wilderness the next morning.

When my father got up in the morning and went to the tent door, he was amazed to find on the ground a round ball of remarkable craftsmanship, made of fine brass. Inside the ball were two spindles, and one of them pointed the direction we should travel in the wilderness.
We gathered everything we needed to carry into the wilderness, along with the rest of our provisions the Lord had given us. We also took seeds of every kind to bring into the wilderness.
We took our tents and departed into the wilderness, crossing the river Laman.
We traveled for four days in a nearly south-southeast direction, then pitched our tents again. We called the place Shazer.

We took our bows and arrows and went into the wilderness to hunt food for our families. After hunting food, we returned to our families at the place of Shazer. Then we traveled on in the same direction, keeping to the most fertile parts of the wilderness, which were near the borders of the Red Sea.
We traveled for many days, hunting food along the way with our bows, arrows, stones, and slings.
We followed the directions of the ball, which led us through the more fertile parts of the wilderness.
After traveling many days, we pitched our tents for a while so we could rest and find food for our families.
As I, Nephi, went out to hunt food, I broke my bow, which was made of fine steel. After I broke my bow, my brothers were angry with me because of the loss, since we could not get food.
We returned to our families without food, and being very tired from traveling, they suffered greatly from lack of food.

Laman, Lemuel, and the sons of Ishmael began to complain bitterly about their suffering and hardships in the wilderness. My father also began to complain against the Lord his God. They were all extremely sorrowful and murmured against the Lord.
I, Nephi, was also suffering along with my brothers because of the loss of my bow, and their bows had lost their spring. It became extremely difficult to get any food.
I, Nephi, spoke many words to my brothers because they had hardened their hearts again and were complaining against the Lord their God.
I, Nephi, made a bow out of wood and an arrow from a straight stick. I armed myself with a bow and arrow, and a sling and stones. Then I asked my father, "Where should I go to find food?"
He prayed to the Lord, because they had humbled themselves because of my words—for I had spoken many things to them with great energy and feeling.
The voice of the Lord came to my father, and he was truly corrected for his complaining against the Lord, so much that he was brought down into deep sorrow.
The voice of the Lord told him, "Look at the ball and read the things written on it."
When my father saw what was written on the ball, he feared and trembled greatly, and so did my brothers, the sons of Ishmael, and our wives.
I, Nephi, saw that the pointers in the ball worked according to the faith, diligence, and attention we gave to them.

New writing also appeared on the ball, which was easy to read. It helped us understand the ways of the Lord, and it changed from time to time according to the faith and diligence we showed. And so we see that the Lord can bring about great things through small means.
I, Nephi, went up to the top of the mountain, following the directions given on the ball.
I hunted and killed wild animals, enough to provide food for our families.
I returned to our tents, carrying the animals I had killed. When they saw that I had gotten food, their joy was tremendous! They humbled themselves before the Lord and gave thanks to him.
We continued our journey, traveling in nearly the same direction as before. After traveling many days, we pitched our tents again to rest for a while.
Ishmael died and was buried in the place called Nahom.

The daughters of Ishmael mourned deeply because of the loss of their father and because of their hardships in the wilderness. They complained against my father for bringing them out of Jerusalem, saying, "Our father is dead. We have wandered so long in the wilderness and suffered so much—affliction, hunger, thirst, and exhaustion. After all this suffering, we are going to die of hunger in the wilderness."
They complained against my father and against me, and they wanted to go back to Jerusalem.
Laman said to Lemuel and the sons of Ishmael, "Let us kill our father and also our brother Nephi, who has taken it upon himself to be our ruler and teacher, even though we are his older brothers."

"He claims that the Lord has spoken to him and that angels have visited him. But we know he is lying to us. He does these things through his clever tricks to deceive us, probably planning to lead us into some strange wilderness. After leading us away, he plans to make himself king and ruler over us, to do whatever he wants with us." This is how Laman stirred up their hearts to anger.

The Lord was with us, and the voice of the Lord came and spoke many words to them and corrected them severely. After being corrected by the voice of the Lord, they turned away from their anger and repented of their sins, so the Lord blessed us again with food and we did not perish.

17.text: We continued our journey in the wilderness, traveling in a nearly eastward direction from then on. We traveled through much hardship in the wilderness, and our women gave birth to children in the wilderness.

The blessings of the Lord upon us were so great that while we lived on raw meat in the wilderness, our women had plenty of milk for their children and were strong, even like the men. They began to endure the journey without complaining.

And so we see that the commandments of God must be fulfilled. If people keep God's commandments, he nourishes them, strengthens them, and provides ways for them to accomplish what he has commanded. He provided for us while we traveled in the wilderness.
We traveled in the wilderness for many years—eight years in all.

We came to the land we called Bountiful because of its abundant fruit and wild honey. All these things were prepared by the Lord so we would not perish. We saw the sea, which we called Irreantum, meaning "many waters."

We pitched our tents by the seashore. Even though we had suffered many hardships and difficulties—so many that we cannot write them all—we were extremely glad when we reached the seashore. We called the place Bountiful because of its abundant fruit.
After I, Nephi, had been in the land of Bountiful for many days, the voice of the Lord came to me, saying, "Get up and go to the mountain." So I went up into the mountain and cried out to the Lord.
The Lord said to me, "You will build a ship, in the way I will show you, so that I can carry your people across these waters."
I said, "Lord, where should I go to find ore to melt so I can make tools to build the ship in the way you have shown me?"
The Lord told me where to go to find ore so I could make tools.
I, Nephi, made a bellows from animal skins to blow the fire. After I made the bellows, I struck two stones together to make fire.
Until then, the Lord had not allowed us to make much fire as we traveled in the wilderness, for he said, "I will make your food sweet so that you do not need to cook it."

"I will also be your light in the wilderness, and I will prepare the way before you, if you will keep my commandments. As long as you keep my commandments, you will be led toward the promised land, and you will know that it is I who lead you."
The Lord also said, "After you arrive in the promised land, you will know that I, the Lord, am God, and that I, the Lord, delivered you from destruction and brought you out of the land of Jerusalem."
Therefore, I, Nephi, strived to keep the commandments of the Lord, and I urged my brothers to be faithful and diligent.
I made tools from the ore I melted out of the rock.
When my brothers saw that I was about to build a ship, they began to complain, saying, "Our brother is a fool! He thinks he can build a ship and cross these great waters."
My brothers complained against me and refused to work because they did not believe I could build a ship. They would not believe I had been taught by the Lord.

I, Nephi, was extremely sorrowful because of the hardness of their hearts. When they saw that I was becoming sad, they were glad and rejoiced over me, saying, "We knew you could not build a ship because you lack good judgment. You cannot accomplish such a great work."

"You are just like our father, led away by the foolish imaginations of his heart. He led us out of Jerusalem, and we have wandered in the wilderness for many years. Our women have worked hard while pregnant, bearing children in the wilderness and suffering everything short of death. It would have been better for them to die before leaving Jerusalem than to suffer these hardships."
"We have suffered in the wilderness all these years when we could have been enjoying our possessions and our inheritance. We could have been happy."

"We know that the people in Jerusalem were righteous because they kept the laws and judgments of the Lord and all his commandments according to the law of Moses. Therefore, we know they are a righteous people. But our father judged them and led us away because we listened to him. Our brother is just like him." This is how my brothers murmured and complained against us.
I, Nephi, said to them, "Do you think our ancestors, the children of Israel, would have been led out of the hands of the Egyptians if they had not listened to the words of the Lord?"
"Do you think they would have been led out of slavery if the Lord had not commanded Moses to lead them out?"

"You know that the children of Israel were in slavery. You know they were burdened with heavy tasks that were hard to bear. So you know it must have been a good thing for them to be brought out of slavery."
"You know that Moses was commanded by the Lord to do that great work. You know that by his word, the waters of the Red Sea were divided in two, and the people passed through on dry ground."
"But you know that the Egyptians—the armies of Pharaoh—were drowned in the Red Sea."
"You also know that they were fed with manna in the wilderness."
"You also know that Moses, by his word and the power of God in him, struck the rock and water came out so the children of Israel could quench their thirst."

"Even though they were being led by the Lord their God and Redeemer, who went before them, leading them by day and giving them light at night, and doing everything for them that was needed, they still hardened their hearts, blinded their minds, and rebelled against Moses and against the true and living God."
"According to his word, he did destroy them; according to his word, he did lead them; according to his word, he did all things for them. Nothing was done except by his word."
"After they crossed the river Jordan, he made them powerful enough to drive out the people of the land and scatter them to destruction."
"Now, do you think that the people of this land—the promised land—who were driven out by our ancestors, were righteous? I tell you, no."
"Do you think our ancestors would have been more favored than them if they had been righteous? I tell you, no."

"The Lord values all people equally. The one who is righteous is favored by God. But these people had rejected every word of God and were fully ripe in wickedness. The full wrath of God was upon them. The Lord cursed the land against them and blessed it for our ancestors—he cursed it against them to their destruction and blessed it for our ancestors so they could have power over it."
"The Lord created the earth to be inhabited, and he created his children to possess it."
"He raises up righteous nations and destroys wicked nations."
"He leads the righteous to precious lands and destroys the wicked, cursing the land against them for their sakes."
"He rules on high in the heavens, for it is his throne, and this earth is his footstool."

"He loves those who accept him as their God. He loved our fathers and made covenants with them—Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. He remembered the covenants he had made, and therefore he brought them out of the land of Egypt."

"He disciplined them in the wilderness with his rod because they hardened their hearts, just as you have. The Lord corrected them because of their wickedness. He sent fiery flying serpents among them, and after they were bitten, he prepared a way for them to be healed. All they had to do was look. But because the solution was so simple, many of them died instead."
"They kept hardening their hearts and rebelled against Moses and against God. Yet you know they were led by his matchless power into the promised land."

"Now, after all these things, the time has come that the people of Jerusalem have become wicked, nearly to the point of ripeness for destruction. I believe they are at this very time about to be destroyed, except for a few who will be taken into captivity."

"This is why the Lord commanded our father to leave for the wilderness. The Jews also tried to take his life, and you also have tried to take his life. Therefore, you are murderers in your hearts and are just like them."

"You are quick to do wrong but slow to remember the Lord your God. You have seen an angel, and he spoke to you. You have heard the Lord's voice from time to time. He has spoken to you in a still, small voice, but you were past feeling and could not feel his words. So he spoke to you like the voice of thunder, which made the earth shake as if it would split apart."

"You also know that by the power of his almighty word, he can cause the earth to pass away. You know that by his word, he can make rough places smooth, and smooth places will be broken up. Then why can you be so hard in your hearts?"
"My soul is torn with anguish because of you, and my heart is pained. I fear you will be cast off forever. I am so full of the Spirit of God that my body has no strength."
