When I had spoken these words, they were angry with me and wanted to throw me into the sea. As they came to grab me, I said to them, "In the name of the Almighty God, I command you not to touch me, for I am filled with the power of God, even to the point that it consumes my flesh. Whoever lays hands on me will wither like a dried reed and be as nothing before the power of God, for God will strike him."
I, Nephi, told them they should stop complaining against their father and should not refuse to work for me, because God had commanded me to build a ship.
I said to them, "If God had commanded me to do anything, I could do it. If he commanded me to say to this water, 'Become earth,' it would become earth. And if I said it, it would be done."
"Since the Lord has such great power and has performed so many miracles among the people, how can he not teach me to build a ship?"

I, Nephi, said many things to my brothers until they were silenced and could not argue against me. They did not dare lay their hands on me or even touch me with their fingers for many days, fearing they would wither before me, so powerful was the Spirit of God working upon them.
The Lord said to me, "Stretch out your hand again to your brothers, and they will not wither before you, but I will give them a shock, says the Lord, so they will know that I am the Lord their God."
I stretched out my hand to my brothers, and they did not wither, but the Lord shook them, just as he had said.

Then they said, "We know for certain that the Lord is with you, because we know it is the power of the Lord that has shaken us." They fell down before me and were about to worship me, but I would not let them, saying, "I am your brother—your younger brother. Worship the Lord your God, and honor your father and mother, so your days may be long in the land the Lord your God will give you."

18.text: They worshipped the Lord and worked with me. We crafted timbers with remarkable skill. The Lord showed me from time to time how I should work the timbers of the ship.
I, Nephi, did not build with timbers the way people normally do, nor did I build the ship in the usual manner. I built it the way the Lord had shown me, so it was not made in the way of men.
I, Nephi, went to the mountain often, and I prayed often to the Lord. The Lord showed me great things.
After I finished the ship, according to the word of the Lord, my brothers saw that it was good and the workmanship was extremely fine. So they humbled themselves again before the Lord.
The voice of the Lord told my father that we should get up and go down to the ship.

The next day, after we had prepared everything—plenty of fruit, meat from the wilderness, honey in abundance, and provisions as the Lord had commanded us—we went down to the ship with all our belongings and seeds, everything we had brought with us, each person according to their age. So we all went into the ship with our wives and children.
My father had two more sons born in the wilderness: the older one was named Jacob and the younger Joseph.
After we had all gone into the ship and loaded our provisions and everything we had been commanded to bring, we set sail and were driven by the wind toward the promised land.

After being driven by the wind for many days, my brothers, the sons of Ishmael, and their wives began to celebrate wildly. They started dancing, singing, and speaking very rudely—so much that they forgot what power had brought them there. They became extremely disrespectful.

I, Nephi, became very afraid that the Lord would be angry with us and strike us because of our wickedness, and we would be swallowed up in the sea. So I, Nephi, began to speak to them with great seriousness. But they were angry with me, saying, "We will not let our younger brother rule over us."
Laman and Lemuel grabbed me and tied me up with ropes, treating me very harshly. But the Lord allowed it so that he could show his power and fulfill the words he had spoken about the wicked.
After they had bound me so I could not move, the compass that the Lord had prepared stopped working.

They did not know which way to steer the ship, so a great and terrible storm arose and drove us backward on the waters for three days. They became extremely frightened that they would drown in the sea, but they still did not untie me.
On the fourth day of being driven backward, the storm became extremely fierce.

We were about to be swallowed up in the depths of the sea. After we had been driven back for four days, my brothers began to see that the judgments of God were upon them and that they would die unless they repented. So they came to me and untied the ropes from my wrists. My wrists had swollen terribly, and my ankles were very swollen too, and the soreness was great.
Nevertheless, I looked to my God and praised him all day long. I did not complain against the Lord because of my suffering.

My father, Lehi, had said many things to them and to the sons of Ishmael, but they threatened anyone who spoke up for me. My parents, being old and having suffered much grief because of their children, were brought down onto their sick beds.

Because of their grief and deep sorrow, and the wickedness of my brothers, they were nearly at the point of death. Their gray hair was about to be laid low in the dust—they were close to being buried with sorrow in a watery grave.

Jacob and Joseph, being young and needing much care, were grieved because of their mother's suffering. My wife, with her tears and prayers, and my children too, could not soften my brothers' hearts enough to untie me.

Nothing except the power of God threatening them with destruction could soften their hearts. When they saw they were about to be swallowed up in the depths of the sea, they repented of what they had done and untied me.
After they untied me, I took the compass and it worked in whatever direction I wanted. I prayed to the Lord, and after I prayed, the winds stopped, the storm ceased, and there was a great calm.
I, Nephi, guided the ship, and we sailed again toward the promised land.
After we had sailed for many days, we arrived at the promised land. We went ashore, pitched our tents, and called it the promised land.
We began to work the soil and plant seeds—all the seeds we had brought from Jerusalem. They grew extremely well, so we were abundantly blessed.

As we explored the promised land, we found animals of every kind in the forests—cows, oxen, donkeys, horses, goats, wild goats, and all kinds of wild animals useful to people. We also found all kinds of ore, including gold, silver, and copper.

19.text: The Lord commanded me, so I made plates of ore on which to engrave the record of my people. On the plates I made, I engraved the record of my father, our travels in the wilderness, and the prophecies of my father. I also engraved many of my own prophecies on them.

I did not know at the time I made them that the Lord would command me to make these particular plates. Therefore, the record of my father, the genealogy of his ancestors, and most of our experiences in the wilderness are engraved on those first plates I mentioned. The things that happened before I made these plates are described in more detail on the first plates.

After I made these plates by commandment, I, Nephi, received a commandment that the ministry and prophecies—the more plain and precious parts—should be written on these plates. The things written on them should be kept for the instruction of my people who would possess the land, and for other wise purposes known to the Lord.

I, Nephi, made a record on the other plates that gives an account—a more detailed account—of the wars, conflicts, and destruction of my people. I did this and instructed my people what to do after I was gone, and that these plates should be passed down from generation to generation, from prophet to prophet, until further commandments from the Lord.
An account of how I made these plates will be given later. For now, I continue with what I have been saying, so that the more sacred things may be preserved for my people's knowledge.

I do not write anything on these plates unless I consider it sacred. If I make mistakes, others in the past made mistakes too. Not that I would excuse myself because of others, but because of the weakness in me according to the flesh, I would excuse myself.

Some people consider things of great worth to both body and soul, while others treat those same things as worthless and trample them underfoot. People even trample the very God of Israel underfoot. I say trample underfoot, but to put it another way—they treat him as worthless and do not listen to the voice of his counsels.
He will come, according to the words of the angel, six hundred years from the time my father left Jerusalem.

The world, because of their wickedness, will consider him worthless. They will whip him, and he will endure it. They will strike him, and he will endure it. They will spit on him, and he will endure it, because of his loving kindness and patience toward the people.

The God of our fathers, who led them out of Egypt and bondage and preserved them in the wilderness—the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—gives himself, according to the words of the angel, as a man into the hands of wicked men, to be lifted up according to the words of Zenock, to be crucified according to the words of Neum, and to be buried in a tomb according to the words of Zenos. Zenos spoke of three days of darkness as a sign of his death to those who live on the islands of the sea, especially to those of the house of Israel.

The prophet spoke: "The Lord God will surely visit all the house of Israel on that day—some with his voice, because of their righteousness, bringing them great joy and salvation, and others with thundering and lightning of his power, by storm, fire, smoke, darkness, earthquakes, and mountains being carried up."

All these things must surely come, says the prophet Zenos. The rocks of the earth must split apart, and because of the groaning of the earth, many kings of the islands of the sea will be moved by the Spirit of God to cry out, "The God of nature suffers!"

As for those in Jerusalem, says the prophet, they will be punished by all peoples because they crucify the God of Israel and turn their hearts away, rejecting signs, wonders, and the power and glory of the God of Israel.
Because they turn their hearts away, says the prophet, and have despised the Holy One of Israel, they will wander in the flesh, perish, and become a mockery and a byword, hated among all nations.
Nevertheless, when the day comes, says the prophet, that they no longer turn their hearts against the Holy One of Israel, then he will remember the covenants he made to their fathers.

Then he will remember the islands of the sea. "All the people who are of the house of Israel, I will gather in," says the Lord, according to the words of the prophet Zenos, "from the four corners of the earth."
"All the earth will see the salvation of the Lord," says the prophet. "Every nation, family, language, and people will be blessed."
I, Nephi, have written these things for my people, hoping I might persuade them to remember the Lord their Redeemer.
Therefore, I speak to all the house of Israel, if they should ever receive these things.

I have spiritual experiences that exhaust me until all my joints are weak—especially concerning those in Jerusalem. If the Lord had not been merciful enough to show me about them, as he showed the prophets of old, I would have perished too.

He surely showed the ancient prophets all things concerning them, and he also showed many prophets things concerning us. Therefore, we must know about them because they are written on the plates of brass.
I, Nephi, taught my brothers these things. I read many things to them from the plates of brass so they could learn about the Lord's doings in other lands among ancient peoples.

I read many things to them from the books of Moses. But to more fully persuade them to believe in the Lord their Redeemer, I read to them from the prophet Isaiah, for I applied all scriptures to ourselves so they could be for our benefit and learning.

I said to them, "Listen to the words of the prophet, you who are a remnant of the house of Israel—a branch that has been broken off. Listen to the words of the prophet, which were written to all the house of Israel, and apply them to yourselves, so you may have hope just like your relatives from whom you have been separated. This is how the prophet wrote."

20.text: Listen and hear this, O house of Jacob, you who are called by the name of Israel and have come out of the waters of Judah—or out of the waters of baptism—who swear by the name of the Lord and mention the God of Israel, yet do not swear in truth or righteousness.
Nevertheless, they call themselves people of the holy city, but they do not rely on the God of Israel, who is the Lord of Hosts. Yes, the Lord of Hosts is his name.
"I declared the former things from the beginning. They went out of my mouth, and I revealed them. I revealed them suddenly."
"I did it because I knew you are stubborn, and your neck is like an iron bar, and your forehead is like brass."
"I declared things to you from the very beginning. Before they happened, I showed them to you, so that you would not say, 'My idol did these things; my carved image or my cast image commanded them.'"
"You have seen and heard all this. Will you not acknowledge it? I have shown you new things from this time—hidden things that you did not know."
"They are created now, not from the beginning. Before today, you had not heard of them, so that you would not say, 'I already knew that.'"
"You had not heard, and you did not know. From that time, your ear was not open, because I knew you would deal very treacherously. You have been called a transgressor from birth."
"Nevertheless, for the sake of my name, I will hold back my anger, and for my praise, I will restrain myself from cutting you off."
"I have refined you and chosen you in the furnace of affliction."
"For my own sake—yes, for my own sake—I will do this, for I will not allow my name to be dishonored, and I will not give my glory to another."
"Listen to me, O Jacob and Israel, my called one. I am he. I am the first, and I am also the last."
"My hand laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand stretched out the heavens. When I call to them, they stand up together."

"All of you, gather together and listen. Who among them has declared these things? The Lord has loved him, and he will fulfill his word declared through them. He will carry out his purpose against Babylon, and his arm will come upon the Chaldeans."
"The Lord also says: I, the Lord, have spoken. I have called him to declare; I have brought him, and he will succeed in his way."
"Come near to me. I have not spoken in secret. From the beginning, from the time it was declared, I have spoken. And the Lord God and his Spirit have sent me."
Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: "I have sent him—the Lord your God, who teaches you to prosper and leads you in the way you should go—has done this."
"If only you had listened to my commandments! Then your peace would have been like a river, and your righteousness like the waves of the sea."
"Your descendants would have been as numerous as the sand, and the children of your body like the grains of sand. Their name would not have been cut off or destroyed from before me."
"Leave Babylon! Flee from the Chaldeans! Declare it with a voice of singing! Announce it and spread it to the end of the earth! Say, 'The Lord has redeemed his servant Jacob.'"
"They did not go thirsty when he led them through the deserts. He made water flow from the rock for them. He split the rock, and the waters gushed out."
"Even though he has done all this and greater things, there is no peace for the wicked," says the Lord.

21.text: And again: "Listen, O house of Israel, all you who have been broken off and driven out because of the wickedness of the leaders of my people—all you who are broken off, scattered abroad, and are of my people, O house of Israel. Listen, O islands, and pay attention, you people from far away. The Lord called me from the womb; from my mother's body he spoke my name."
"He has made my mouth like a sharp sword. In the shadow of his hand he has hidden me. He has made me a polished arrow; in his quiver he has hidden me."
"He said to me, 'You are my servant, O Israel, in whom I will be glorified.'"
"Then I said, 'I have labored in vain; I have spent my strength for nothing and to no purpose. Yet surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my reward is with my God.'"

"Now the Lord says—he who formed me from the womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob back to him—though Israel is not yet gathered, I will be honored in the eyes of the Lord, and my God will be my strength."

"He said, 'It is too small a thing for you to be my servant just to restore the tribes of Jacob and bring back the preserved of Israel. I will also make you a light to the Gentiles, so that you may be my salvation to the ends of the earth.'"

Thus says the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel, his Holy One, to him whom man despises, to him whom the nations hate, to the servant of rulers: "Kings will see and stand up, and princes will worship, because of the Lord, who is faithful."

Thus says the Lord: "At the right time I have heard you, O islands of the sea, and in the day of salvation I have helped you. I will preserve you and give you my servant as a covenant for the people, to rebuild the earth and restore the desolate lands."
"So that you may say to the prisoners, 'Go free!' and to those in darkness, 'Come out and show yourselves!' They will feed along the roads, and they will find pasture in every high place."
"They will not hunger or thirst, and neither heat nor sun will strike them, for he who has mercy on them will lead them and guide them to springs of water."
"I will turn all my mountains into roads, and my highways will be raised up."
"Then, O house of Israel, these will come from far away—some from the north and west, and some from the land of Sinim."

"Sing, O heavens! Be joyful, O earth, for the feet of those in the east will be established! Break forth in singing, O mountains, for they will be struck down no more! The Lord has comforted his people and will have mercy on his afflicted ones."
"But Zion has said, 'The Lord has forsaken me; my Lord has forgotten me.' But he will show that he has not."
"Can a woman forget her nursing child and have no compassion for the son she bore? Even if she could forget, I will not forget you, O house of Israel."
"I have engraved you on the palms of my hands. Your walls are continually before me."
"Your children will come quickly to defend you, and those who laid you waste will leave you."
"Lift up your eyes and look around. All these are gathering and coming to you. As surely as I live," says the Lord, "you will wear them all like an ornament and bind them on like a bride."
"Your ruined and desolate places, and your destroyed land, will soon be too small for your inhabitants, and those who devoured you will be far away."
"The children born after you lost the first ones will say in your hearing, 'This place is too crowded for me. Make room for me to live.'"

"Then you will say in your heart, 'Who gave me all these children? I had lost my children and was alone, a captive, wandering back and forth. Who raised these? I was left all alone. Where did they come from?'"
Thus says the Lord God: "I will raise my hand to the Gentiles and set up my banner for the peoples. They will carry your sons in their arms, and your daughters will be carried on their shoulders."

"Kings will be your foster fathers and their queens your nursing mothers. They will bow before you with their faces to the ground and lick the dust at your feet. Then you will know that I am the Lord, for those who wait for me will not be put to shame."
"Can prey be taken from the mighty warrior, or captives be rescued from the conqueror?"

"But thus says the Lord: 'Even the captives of the mighty will be taken away, and the prey of the conqueror will be rescued. I will fight against those who fight against you, and I will save your children.'"

"I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh, and they will be drunk on their own blood as if on sweet wine. Then all people will know that I, the Lord, am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob."

22.text: After I, Nephi, had read these things that were engraved on the plates of brass, my brothers came to me and asked, "What do these things mean? Should they be understood as spiritual things that will happen according to the spirit and not the flesh?"

I, Nephi, said to them, "These things were revealed to the prophet by the voice of the Spirit, for all things are made known to the prophets by the Spirit. These things will happen to people according to the flesh."
"The things I have read relate to things both physical and spiritual, because it appears that the house of Israel will eventually be scattered across the whole earth and among all nations."

"Many are already lost from the knowledge of those in Jerusalem. Most of the tribes have been led away. They are scattered across the islands of the sea, and where they are, none of us knows, except that we know they have been led away."

"Since they have been led away, these things have been prophesied about them, and also about all who will be scattered and confused in the future because of the Holy One of Israel. They will harden their hearts against him, and therefore they will be scattered among all nations and hated by all people."

"Nevertheless, after they are cared for by the Gentiles, and the Lord has raised his hand over the Gentiles and set them up as a standard, and their children have been carried in their arms and daughters on their shoulders—these things that were spoken of are literal and physical. These are the covenants of the Lord with our fathers, and it applies to us in the days to come, and also to all our relatives who are of the house of Israel."

"It means that the time will come when, after all the house of Israel has been scattered and confused, the Lord God will raise up a mighty nation among the Gentiles on the face of this land, and our descendants will be scattered by them."

"After our descendants are scattered, the Lord God will do a marvelous work among the Gentiles that will be of great value to our descendants. It is compared to their being nourished by the Gentiles and being carried in their arms and on their shoulders."

"It will also be of value to the Gentiles—and not only to the Gentiles but to all the house of Israel—making known the covenants of the Father of heaven with Abraham, who said, 'Through your descendants, all the families of the earth will be blessed.'"
"I want you to know, my brothers, that all the families of the earth cannot be blessed unless the Lord reveals his power openly before the nations."
"Therefore, the Lord God will reveal his power openly before all nations, bringing about his covenants and his gospel for those of the house of Israel."

"He will bring them out of captivity again, and they will be gathered to the lands of their inheritance. They will be brought out of obscurity and darkness, and they will know that the Lord is their Savior and their Redeemer, the Mighty One of Israel."

"The blood of that great and wicked church, the whore of all the earth, will turn upon their own heads. They will war among themselves, and the sword of their own hands will fall upon their own heads. They will be drunk with their own blood."

"Every nation that fights against you, O house of Israel, will be turned against each other. They will fall into the pit they dug to trap the people of the Lord. All who fight against Zion will be destroyed, and that great whore who has twisted the right ways of the Lord—that great and wicked church—will crumble to dust, and great will be its fall."

"For the prophet says the time is coming quickly when Satan will have no more power over people's hearts. The day is coming soon when all the proud and wicked will be like stubble, and the day will come when they must be burned."
"The time is coming soon when the fulness of God's wrath will be poured out upon all the people, for he will not allow the wicked to destroy the righteous."

"He will preserve the righteous by his power, even if the fulness of his wrath must come and the righteous must be preserved through the destruction of their enemies by fire. Therefore, the righteous do not need to fear, for the prophet says they will be saved, even if by fire."

"My brothers, I tell you that these things must happen soon—blood, fire, and clouds of smoke must come upon the face of this earth. It will come to people in the flesh if they harden their hearts against the Holy One of Israel."
"The righteous will not perish, for the time will surely come when all who fight against Zion will be cut off."

"The Lord will surely prepare a way for his people, fulfilling the words of Moses, who said, 'The Lord your God will raise up a prophet for you, like me. You must listen to him in everything he says to you. Everyone who will not listen to that prophet will be cut off from among the people.'"
"Now I, Nephi, declare to you that this prophet Moses spoke of was the Holy One of Israel. Therefore, he will execute judgment in righteousness."
"The righteous do not need to fear, for they will not be put to shame. But the kingdom of the devil, which will be built up among the people and established among those in the flesh—"

"The time will come quickly when all churches built to get money, all who are built up to get power over people, those built to become popular in the world, those who seek the pleasures of the flesh and the things of the world, and those who do every kind of wickedness—in short, all who belong to the kingdom of the devil need to fear, tremble, and quake. They are the ones who must be brought low in the dust. They must be consumed like stubble. This is according to the words of the prophet."
"The time is coming quickly when the righteous will be nourished like calves in the stall, and the Holy One of Israel will reign in dominion, might, power, and great glory."

"He gathers his children from the four corners of the earth. He counts his sheep, and they know him. There will be one fold and one shepherd. He will feed his sheep, and in him they will find pasture."

"Because of the righteousness of his people, Satan will have no power. He will not be able to act for many years because he has no power over people's hearts, for they live in righteousness, and the Holy One of Israel reigns."
"Now I, Nephi, say to you that all these things must come to pass in the flesh."
"But all nations, peoples, languages, and races will dwell safely in the Holy One of Israel if they will repent."
"Now I, Nephi, bring this to a close, for I dare not speak further yet about these things."
"My brothers, I want you to consider that the things written on the plates of brass are true, and they testify that a person must be obedient to the commandments of God."

"You should not think that my father and I are the only ones who have testified and taught these things. If you are obedient to the commandments and endure to the end, you will be saved at the last day. And so it is. Amen."
